title: pyrrosia eleagnifolia
intro: Pyrrosia eleagnifolia, also known as Elephant Ear or Elephant Foot, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia, and its rhizome is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The rhizome of Pyrrosia eleagnifolia is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the rhizome can be made into a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections.
